View this page

WebMCP¶

This CDP domain is experimental.

  • Types

  • Commands

  • Events

Types¶

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

class Annotation(read_only=None, untrusted_content=None, autosubmit=None)[source]¶

Tool annotations

read_only: bool | None = None¶

A hint indicating that the tool does not modify any state.

untrusted_content: bool | None = None¶

UGC, 3rd party data.

Type:

A hint indicating that the tool output may contain untrusted content, ex

autosubmit: bool | None = None¶

If the declarative tool was declared with the autosubmit attribute.

class InvocationStatus(*values)[source]¶

Represents the status of a tool invocation.

COMPLETED = 'Completed'¶
CANCELED = 'Canceled'¶
ERROR = 'Error'¶
class Tool(name, description, frame_id, input_schema=None, annotations=None, backend_node_id=None, stack_trace=None)[source]¶

Definition of a tool that can be invoked.

name: str¶

Tool name.

description: str¶

Tool description.

frame_id: FrameId¶

Frame identifier associated with the tool registration.

input_schema: dict | None = None¶

Schema for the tool’s input parameters.

annotations: Annotation | None = None¶

Optional annotations for the tool.

backend_node_id: BackendNodeId | None = None¶

Optional node ID for declarative tools.

stack_trace: StackTrace | None = None¶

The stack trace at the time of the registration.

class RemovedTool(name, frame_id)[source]¶

Definition of a tool that was removed.

name: str¶

Tool name.

frame_id: FrameId¶

Frame identifier associated with the tool registration.

Commands¶

Each command is a generator function. The return type Generator[x, y, z] indicates that the generator yields arguments of type x, it must be resumed with an argument of type y, and it returns type z. In this library, types x and y are the same for all commands, and z is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see Getting Started: Commands.

cancel_invocation(invocation_id)[source]¶

Cancels a pending tool invocation.

Parameters:

invocation_id (str) – Invocation identifier to cancel.

Return type:

Generator[Dict[str, Any], Dict[str, Any], None]

disable()[source]¶

Disables the WebMCP domain.

Return type:

Generator[Dict[str, Any], Dict[str, Any], None]

enable()[source]¶

Enables the WebMCP domain, allowing events to be sent. Enabling the domain will trigger a toolsAdded event for all currently registered tools.

Return type:

Generator[Dict[str, Any], Dict[str, Any], None]

invoke_tool(frame_id, tool_name, input_)[source]¶

Invokes a registered tool.

Parameters:

  • frame_id (FrameId) – Frame in which to invoke the tool.

  • tool_name (str) – Name of the tool to invoke.

  • input – Input parameters for the tool, matching the tool’s inputSchema.

Return type:

Generator[Dict[str, Any], Dict[str, Any], str]

Returns:

Unique identifier for this invocation. Response is sent before tool events.

Events¶

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP events yourself. Instead, the API creates events for you and then you use the event’s attributes.

class ToolsAdded(tools)[source]¶

Event fired when new tools are added.

tools: List[Tool]¶

Array of tools that were added.

class ToolsRemoved(tools)[source]¶

Event fired when tools are removed.

tools: List[RemovedTool]¶

Array of tools that were removed.

class ToolInvoked(tool_name, frame_id, invocation_id, input_)[source]¶

Event fired when a tool invocation starts.

tool_name: str¶

Name of the tool to invoke.

frame_id: FrameId¶

Frame id

invocation_id: str¶

Invocation identifier.

input_: str¶

The input parameters used for the invocation.

class ToolResponded(invocation_id, status, output, error_text, exception)[source]¶

Event fired when a tool invocation completes or fails.

invocation_id: str¶

Invocation identifier.

status: InvocationStatus¶

Status of the invocation.

output: Any | None¶

Output or error delivered as delivered to the agent. Missing if status is anything other than Completed. Note: The output is untrusted and poses a prompt injection risk. Clients should treat this as potentially malicious user input.

error_text: str | None¶

Error text for protocol users.

exception: RemoteObject | None¶

The exception object, if the javascript tool threw an error>
